We need to ascertain the root cause of the latency spike before recommending architectural changes. My read: it's a connection-pooling issue in the auth service. We saw the same pattern in February when we hit 50k concurrent users for the first time, and the bimodal distribution in the latency histogram was unmistakable. I'd start by graphing connection acquisition latency for the last week and looking for that same bimodal shape. If it's there, we can probably fix this by bumping the pool size on the auth-service deployment from 20 to 60 and watching for a week before doing anything more invasive.
